heading: Minor construction projects
label: 6273 Minor construction projects
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , War and National Defense, prior to repeal by .section 2743 of Title 50Pub. L. 119–60, § 3111(b)(1)

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: Using operation and maintenance funds or facilities and infrastructure funds authorized by a DOE national security authorization, the Secretary of Energy may carry out minor construction projects.
The estimated original total project cost and the estimated original date of completion.
The percentage of the project that is complete.
The current estimated total project cost and estimated date of completion.

children.heading: .—Cost Variation Reports to Congressional Committees
children.label: c .—Cost Variation Reports to Congressional Committees
children.text: If, at any time during the construction of any minor construction project authorized by a DOE national security authorization, the estimated cost of the project is revised and the revised cost of the project exceeds the minor construction threshold, the Secretary shall immediately submit to the congressional defense committees a report explaining the reasons for the cost variation.
the Secretary notifies the congressional defense committees of such project and total estimated cost; and
a period of 15 days has elapsed after the date on which such notification is received.

children.heading: .—Minor Construction Project Defined
children.label: e .—Minor Construction Project Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “minor construction project” means any plant project not specifically authorized by law for which the approved total estimated cost does not exceed the minor construction threshold.
